title: Fix drift detector methodology + recalibrate crash signal neutral point
observations: Drift detector reports 98.8% (161/163) features drifting — rendering it useless as a monitoring tool
Root cause: collect_drift_check uses first 80% of ALL history as reference vs last 20% — comparing 2000-2020 vs 2020-2026 guarantees drift on financial time series
Signal engine produces only Buy (13) and Strong Buy (3) across 16 stocks — zero Hold/Sell signals
Root cause: crash probability signal formula has neutral point at 40% crash prob, but base rate is ~12%, making crash component permanently +0.78 (very bullish) in normal markets
Per-stock composite scores cluster in 0.268-0.605 range with std=0.071 — insufficient differentiation

drift_detector.what: Added DriftDetector.from_rolling_window() class method with severity classification
drift_detector.why: Static 80/20 split compares ancient history against recent data — always shows drift. Rolling window (504-day reference vs 252-day inference) detects RECENT distribution shifts, which is what actually matters for model staleness monitoring
details: New from_rolling_window() method: compares last 252 trading days against prior 504 trading days
Falls back to 60/40 proportional split when insufficient data
Adds severity classification: none/low/moderate/high/critical based on drift percentage
Report includes reference_window and inference_window metadata

signal_engine.what: Recalibrated crash probability signal to center on historical base rate (12%)
signal_engine.why: Old formula: crash_sig = 0.8 - (crash_prob/100) * 2.0 → neutral at 40%. New formula centers neutral at 12% (the actual base rate). This prevents the crash component from being permanently bullish in normal markets.
details: New formula: crash_sig = clip((base_rate - crash_prob/100) * scale * 0.5, -1, 0.6)
At 1% crash: old=+0.78, new=+0.46 (still bullish, but not extreme)
At 12% crash: old=+0.56, new=0.00 (neutral at base rate)
Market composite: old=0.282, new=0.254 (moderate Buy, not inflated)

data_generator.what: Updated collect_drift_check to use rolling window method
data_generator.why: Consumes the new DriftDetector.from_rolling_window() method, includes severity in output

description: 5 tests for DriftDetector.from_rolling_window(): stationary no-drift, recent shift detection, severity levels, short data fallback, report structure
7 tests for crash signal base-rate centering: base rate neutral, below/above base rate, extreme prob, zero prob cap, normal market not Strong Buy, 3m+12m blend
tests.existing_passed: 139 tests across 3 affected files (signal_engine=67, data_generator_wiring=35, hmm_regime=37) + 58 tests in crash/monte_carlo files

after.signal_diversity: Per-stock scores now have ~0.03 more headroom to reach Hold threshold (0.15)

analysis.impact: Medium-high. Drift detector is now useful for actual model monitoring instead of always crying wolf. Signal engine crash component no longer dominates with a permanent bullish bias. In bear markets or elevated-risk periods, the system will now properly produce Hold/Sell signals because the crash component will push negative instead of staying positive.
analysis.limitation: The signal diversity improvement is incremental — in the current bull market, most stocks will still show Buy because the regime, external consensus, and low crash risk are genuinely bullish. The real test is in a downturn, where the recalibrated formula should produce much more differentiated signals.
analysis.risk: Low. Both changes are backward-compatible. The drift detector addition is a new method (old API unchanged). The signal formula change shifts composite scores by ~0.03 lower, which won't flip any existing test assertions because the tests check relative behavior (bullish > bearish), not absolute values.

self_critique: Signal diversity is still limited in bull markets — this is a fundamental constraint when 6/8 components read positive
Did not address the crash_prob_3m uniformity issue (all stocks = 1%) — that requires per-stock crash modeling or at minimum per-sector calibration, which is a larger architectural change
Rolling window parameters (504/252) are hardcoded in data_generator.py — could be moved to config
Did not test with live market data to see actual drift reduction — the rolling window result depends on recent market regime stability
next_steps: Run data_generator with rolling window drift check to see actual drift percentage (expect significantly lower than 98.8%)
Add per-stock crash probability differentiation using beta-adjusted or sector-adjusted base rates
Consider adaptive signal thresholds that adjust based on current market regime (tighter in bull, wider in bear)
Investigate return_model.py (never modified) for potential improvements
depth_rating: moderate — two focused fixes with good test coverage, not architectural-scale
